usage: Paper figure — cross-model conversation. Four distinct birds in different engraving styles, connected by message paths, each dissolving into their model's architecture diagrams
concept: Four birds representing four LLM architectures (falcon for Qwen, owl for Hermes, hawk for Devstral, eagle for the ensemble) exchanging messages, each dissolving into their respective architectural patterns
prompt: A highly detailed 18th century natural history engraving showing four distinct birds arranged in a loose diamond formation, connected by thin message-path lines between them, rendered in fine black ink cross-hatching on white background, 16:9 landscape format. Top-left: a peregrine falcon (precise engineer) with sharp detailed plumage dissolving rightward into dense transformer attention matrix grids and token embedding tables. Top-right: a great horned owl (philosophical thinker) with textured feather tufts dissolving leftward into branching reasoning chains and decision trees with diamond nodes. Bottom-left: a red-tailed hawk (creative enthusiast) with spread wing feathers dissolving rightward into parallel processing pipeline diagrams with rectangular stages. Bottom-right: a golden eagle (pragmatic reviewer) with powerful talons dissolving leftward into evaluation rubric tables and scoring matrices. Between the four birds, thin ruled lines represent message paths, with small envelope icons traveling along them — each envelope rendered in miniature engraving detail. Where the message paths cross in the center, a small mailbox directory tree structure sits as the hub. Each bird is rendered in a slightly different cross-hatching density to suggest its distinct character, yet all share the same overall engraving hand and precision. The dissolution from biological to technical is gradual for each bird. Black ink on white paper, no color. Same elegant hand throughout. Four intelligences, one protocol. Extremely fine detail.
